steps.title: Identify the chronological contrast
steps.content: The passage explicitly contrasts Calle Pedro Loza as an earlier project embodying traditional Mediterranean and Mexican styles with the Pizarro Suárez House, which Barragán designed after adopting functionalism and modernism following his 1930s travels.

steps.title: Recognize what he abandoned
steps.content:
B correctly identifies Calle Pedro Loza's style as something Barragán later stopped using.

The traditional ornamental aesthetic represents his pre-modernist approach, which the passage shows he moved beyond.
  • A mistakes it for a transitional work rather than a pre-transition baseline
  • C inverts the timeline
  • D overstates the permanence of its influence


That matches the correct answer — B is correct.
